title: Bridge Essay: Slow breathing and hypoxic challenge: cardiorespiratory consequences and their c

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** (Phase 2) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Breath/Soul to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Slow breathing and hypoxic challenge: cardiorespiratory consequences and their central neural substrates.** — PLoS One. PMID: 25973923. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0127082. Controlled slow breathing (at 6/min, arate frequently adopted during yoga practice) can benefit cardiovascular function, including responses to hypoxia. We tested the neural substrates of cardiorespiratory control in humans during volitional controlled breathing and hypoxic challenge using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI). Twenty healthy volunteers were scanned during paced (slow and normal rate) breathing and during spontaneous breathing of normoxic and hypoxic (13% inspired O2) ai
